content:
@Eryk Kulikowski @Oliver Bertuch @Vera Clemens now that there are three of you on the core team that live in Europe I'm thinking we should move tech hours to be earlier in the day.

If we don't move it, our next tech hours will be at this time (it should show in your timezone): 2026-07-14T15:00:00-04:00

Pretty late for you, right? What would be a better time? It doesn't have to be on Tuesdays!

I assume our business hours are the usual 2026-07-09T08:00:00+02:00 till 2026-07-09T17:00:00+02:00. There is that small overlap between our 14:00 to 17:00 matching up with your business hours. So that is probably the possible time frame if we want to stay within everybody's hours. This might narrow down if we ever add core members from Portugal (-1h) or Latvia (+1h).
That said, the current 21:00 usually/mostly works for me. I'm an :owl: so I don't mind. It's paid work time, too, as per exception from my employer.

I'd be able to join anything with an end time up to 2026-07-14T17:30:00+02:00. Past that, I'm usually not available.

Any day of the week works for me. Only possible conflict in my calendar is the newly added Frontend Group meeting on Thursdays, but you've probably got that on your radar as well :smile:

Here's a thought (that I haven't run by people in my time zone yet!)...

Currently the community call is the first Tuesday of the month at 10am Boston time, 2026-07-07T10:00:00-04:00 this week.

What if tech hours was at the same time, but when the community call is happening we all go to that instead? (Or some of us skip the community call, which is fine.)

That is, we'd still have weekly tech hours except for the week of the community call.
If we we go for 10am in Boston, here's a summary of how the time will bounce around from 3pm to 4pm in Germany and Belgium (sorry!):
@Vera Clemens @Oliver Bertuch @Eryk Kulikowski please look for a new Google Calendar invite for tomorrow at 10am Boston time! This time for you: 2026-07-21T10:00:00-04:00
Minimal grumbling about the earlier time. :smile:
